cohort_season: cohort_season
YYYY-MM format. The calendar month in which these buyers made their first purchase.
spring_planning (Jan-Apr) | long_tail_spring (May-Jun) | preservation (Jul-Sep) | long_tail_fall (Oct) | holiday_gift (Nov-Dec)
Count of unique buyer emails who placed their first-ever Seedwarden order in this month. Source: Etsy API receipts query filtered to first-purchase buyers only.
Count of buyers from this cohort who placed any order (including repeat) during Month 1 of their lifecycle (same month as acquisition). Should equal buyers_acquired.
Always 100.0 — baseline.
Count of buyers from this cohort who placed an order in the second calendar month after acquisition. Source: cohort-analysis-template.sql Section 2.
month_2_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 8%+ for non-gift cohorts.
Count of buyers from this cohort who placed any order in Month 3 of their lifecycle.
month_3_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 15%+ (equivalent to 90-day second purchase rate). This is the primary Phase 2 decision metric.
Count of buyers from this cohort who placed any order in Month 6 of their lifecycle.
month_6_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 10%+ for non-gift cohorts. Gift buyer target: 3%+.
Average cumulative net revenue per buyer in this cohort through Month 1. Net revenue = gross * 0.905 - $0.25. Source: cohort-analysis-template.sql Section 3.
Average cumulative net revenue per buyer through Month 3 (all orders in Months 1-3 summed per buyer / buyers_acquired).
Average cumulative net revenue per buyer through Month 6. This is the primary LTV figure for Phase 2 gate decisions.
The most common assigned cohort in this acquisition month. One of: high_intent_forager | survival_prepper | homesteader | gift_buyer | mixed.
Percentage of this cohort who made a second purchase within 90 days of their first purchase. Target: 20%+. Primary health indicator.
Mean number of days between first and second purchase for buyers in this cohort who made a second purchase. Target: 45-75 days for foragers; 60-90 for homesteaders; 60-120 for preppers.
Most purchased guide as first purchase in this cohort. Source: cohort-analysis-template.sql Section 5.1.
Most purchased guide as second purchase in this cohort (among buyers who made a second purchase). Source: cohort-analysis-template.sql Section 5.2.
Percentage of second purchases that were a different guide category than the first purchase (vs. same category at different price tier). High cross_sell_rate = healthy catalog breadth.
Top traffic acquisition source for this cohort. Source: GA4 traffic acquisition report filtered to the cohort's acquisition month.
Whether this cohort shows elevated repeat purchase activity during a particular season. One of: spring_planning | preservation | holiday_gift | uniform | insufficient_data
Qualitative observations from monthly review. Note anomalies (spike in orders from a single source; a listing going viral; a seasonal event that drove unusual volume).
Jan_Feb_Mar_Apr buyers
Sum of buyers_acquired across all months in this season.
Average of cohort_ltv_m6 for all cohorts acquired in this season.

Average second_purchase_rate_90d_pct for all cohorts acquired in this season. The seasonal pattern that shows the highest repeat rate identifies your highest-LTV acquisition window for Phase 2 ad spend.

Which season do buyers acquired in this season tend to make their repeat purchase? E.g. preservation-acquired buyers may repeat heavily in holiday_gift season. Track this to time email campaigns appropriately.
